summaryText: Guest Speaker Montell Jordan opened by sharing his personal story, including celebrating 30 years of marriage and his recent journey through a prostate cancer diagnosis and recovery. He connected these experiences to the series on character, teaching that godly character is the fruit of the Spirit produced in us. Pastor Montell defined character as our internal moral qualities, distinct from our external personality, and emphasized that true character is revealed under pressure. He presented a three-part framework for Christlike character development: it is established through reliability, maintained through resilience, and remembered through righteousness. Using personal examples, including his wife Kristen's faithful prayer life and his own decision to perform shortly after major surgery, he illustrated what it means to be dependable, to bounce back from adversity with faith, and to seek right standing with God through Jesus Christ alone. The message concluded with an invitation for listeners to surrender their lives to Christ, exchanging their old ways for His reliable, resilient, and righteous character.
mainPoints: Godly character is established through reliability—being trustworthy and consistent in our actions, especially in private commitments like prayer.
Godly character is maintained through resilience—the God-given strength to withstand challenges and bounce back quickly from adversity.
Godly character is remembered through righteousness—receiving right standing with God through faith in Jesus Christ, not through our own efforts.

versesMentioned.text: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, forbearance, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, gentleness and self-control. Against such things there is no law. Those who belong to Christ Jesus have crucified the flesh with its passions and desires.
And we know that in all things God works for the good of those who love him, who have been called according to his purpose.
Let us not become weary in doing good, for at the proper time we will reap a harvest if we do not give up.

Consider it pure joy, my brothers and sisters, whenever you face trials of many kinds, because you know that the testing of your faith produces perseverance. Let perseverance finish its work so that you may be mature and complete, not lacking anything.
The Lord is close to the brokenhearted and saves those who are crushed in spirit.
Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they will be filled.

Now we know that whatever the law says, it says to those who are under the law, so that every mouth may be silenced and the whole world held accountable to God. Therefore no one will be declared righteous in God’s sight by the works of the law; rather, through the law we become conscious of our sin. But now apart from the law the righteousness of God has been made known, to which the Law and the Prophets testify. This righteousness is given through faith in Jesus Christ to all who believe. There is no difference between Jew and Gentile, for all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God, and all are justified freely by his grace through the redemption that came by Christ Jesus. God presented Christ as a sacrifice of atonement, through the shedding of his blood—to be received by faith. He did this to demonstrate his righteousness, because in his forbearance he had left the sins committed beforehand unpunished— he did it to demonstrate his righteousness at the present time, so as to be just and the one who justifies those who have faith in Jesus.

Monday.scripture: Galatians 5:22-23 - 'But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace...'
Monday.action: Identify one 'fruit of the Spirit' you feel is weak in your life. Pray for the Holy Spirit to produce it in you today.
Monday.prayer: Lord, I surrender to your Spirit. Produce your fruit in me, starting with [name the fruit]. Let my character reflect Yours.

Tuesday.scripture: Romans 8:28 - 'And we know that in all things God works for the good...'
Tuesday.action: Recall a recent disappointment. Write down one way you can choose to trust God's reliability in that situation.
Tuesday.prayer: Father, you are reliable in all things. Help me to trust your purpose and your timing, even when I don't understand.

Wednesday.scripture: James 1:2-4 - 'Consider it pure joy...whenever you face trials...'
Wednesday.action: Think of a current challenge. Instead of asking for it to be removed, ask God for the strength and endurance to grow through it.
Wednesday.prayer: God, give me your perspective on my trials. Let my faith be tested so my endurance can grow, making me more like Christ.

Thursday.scripture: Psalm 34:18 - 'The Lord is close to the brokenhearted...'
Thursday.action: In a moment of stress or pressure today, pause and consciously acknowledge God's nearness. Take three deep breaths as a prayer.
Thursday.prayer: Thank you, Lord, for drawing close to me when I feel crushed. I receive your comfort and strength right now.

Friday.scripture: Matthew 5:6 - 'Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness...'
Friday.action: Evaluate one area where you seek approval from people more than approval from God. Make a conscious choice to seek God's 'right standing' instead.
Friday.prayer: Jesus, I hunger to be in right standing with you more than I desire the approval of others. Fill me with your righteousness.

Saturday.scripture: Romans 3:23-24 - 'for all have sinned...and all are justified freely by his grace...'
Saturday.action: Reflect on the truth that righteousness is a gift received through faith, not earned. Thank God specifically for this gift.
Saturday.prayer: Thank you, Jesus, for making me right with God. I receive your gift of righteousness by faith today. I cannot save myself; I trust in you.
